reasoning: The Bulgargaz-BOTAS agreement has become a severe financial burden for Bulgaria, leaving Bulgargaz on the verge of bankruptcy with massive arrears. While there is immense political pressure in Bulgaria to exit the 13-year deal (evidenced by Borissov's recent appeal to Erdogan), terminating the contract triggers a devastating penalty of around BGN 4 billion (approx. $2.2 billion). Bulgarian officials, including the Energy Minister, have repeatedly acknowledged this legal trap, reducing the likelihood of a voluntary unilateral cancellation by Bulgaria. On the other hand, Turkey has consistently shown no willingness to void the lucrative deal. While a unilateral announcement or a default leading BOTAS to formalize termination remains a possibility, the sheer scale of the financial and diplomatic fallout encourages both sides to drag out negotiations rather than force an abrupt rupture. Given the extremely tight window (less than 25 days until July 1, 2025), the probability of an official cancellation announcement occurring in this short timeframe is low.
